BoC Meeting Minutes: Members agreed that the economy was weak, operating in excess supply, and there was slack in the labor market, but the economy was not in recession.
BoC Meeting Minutes: Ahead of the Bank of Canada's June 10th rate decision, governing council members agreed that monetary policy will need to remain nimble.
BoC Meeting Minutes: Members agreed that if the inflation data began to show evidence that inflation pressures were spreading, it would be a signal that monetary policy tightening is warranted
